primary_application: Laser cleaning for decontaminating lead surfaces, such as efficiently removing oxide layers or subtle surface imperfections from lead-based components in battery manufacturing—where thermal ablation helps mitigate corrosion—or radiation shielding setups, leveraging its high density and moderate thermal conductivity for precise, non-contact restoration in industrial settings.
table:
| Property                  | Value                          |
|----------|-----------------------------|
| Atomic Number             | 82                            |
| Density                   | 11.34 g/cm³ at 20°C          |
| Melting Point             | 327.5°C                       |
| Boiling Point             | 1749°C                        |
| Thermal Conductivity      | 34.3 W/m·K at 20°C            |
| Specific Heat Capacity    | 0.129 kJ/kg·K                 |
| Reflectivity (e.g., at 1064 nm for Nd:YAG lasers) | Approximately 30% (varies with wavelength and surface condition) |
